scenario: Rubio 'every chance to succeed' May 27 (CNBC); but White House calls Iran MOU claim 'complete fabrication'. Fresh US strikes in Iran + IRGC targets US air base (May 28; Tasnim). Brent $94.29 confirmed (CNBC May 27 close, -5%); WTI $88.68 confirmed (-5%); TTF €44.00 est. (-5.8%); JKM $18.81 est. (Platts May 22). Ceasefire extension reportedly close; ADNOC CEO: 4 months to 80% Hormuz flow even if conflict ends. Tail 28% (up from 23% on renewed military exchanges).

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat eases (May 27 est.; Brent $94.29 confirmed -5%); White House denial + fresh US strikes reopen tail risk; Qafco force majeure and Cape rerouting persist until Hormuz signed + mine-cleared; fertilizer supply mechanism intact. Tail 28%. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation eases with Brent $94.29 (May 27 confirmed, -5%); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; Cape routing disruption unchanged until Hormuz physically reopens; tail 28%.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs easing with Brent $94.29 (May 27 confirmed); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; Cape routing costs persist until physical Hormuz reopening; tail 28%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO eases with Brent $94.29 (May 27 confirmed, -5%); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; Indonesia B40 mandate structural floor intact; Malaysia June supply gap easing on deal prospect but less certain after White House denial. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure eases marginally; Brent $94.29 (May 27 confirmed); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; Qafco force majeure persists until deal signed + mine-cleared; planting window pressure acute near-term; tail 28%.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha falls with Brent $94.29 (May 27 confirmed, -5%); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; Gulf condensate interdiction persists until Hormuz physically reopens; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Tail 28%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene tracks naphtha lower (May 27 est.; Brent $94.29 confirmed); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks persist until mine-clearing; tail 28%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol eases on Rubio deal optimism (May 27 est.; Brent $94.29 confirmed); but White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; US Iran sanctions waiver expired Apr 19; Qatar force majeure persists; OFAC Economic Fury (May 11) enforcement unchanged; tail 28%. China MTO complexes: feedstock pressure moderating. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea eases (May 27 est.; Brent $94.29 confirmed -5%); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; Qafco fm lifting less certain after White House 'fabrication' denial; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; OFAC Economic Fury (May 11) enforcement unchanged. Tail 28%. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP eases (May 27 est.; Brent $94.29 confirmed -5%); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; Saudi Ma'aden shipping disruption persists until Hormuz physically reopens; wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam acute; tail 28%.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight eases with Brent $94.29 (May 27 confirmed); White House denial + fresh strikes reopen tail; Cape rerouting persists until Hormuz mine-cleared; palm oil plantations: input cost pressure moderating; tail 28%.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI falls on Rubio deal optimism (May 27 est.); White House denial + fresh strikes limit downside; P&I suspension persists until formal mine-clearing begins; physical reopening ~6 months from deal signature. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates fall sharply (May 27 est.; Brent $94.29 confirmed -5%); White House denial + fresh strikes put floor under rates; physical reopening requires mine-clearing (~6 months); P&I restoration conditional; tail (28%) supports elevated rate floor. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI carried (weekly; published Friday May 23; next Friday May 30). Cape rerouting premium to persist until Hormuz physically reopens + P&I restoration + mine-clearing (~6 months even on deal); White House denial sustains uncertainty; tail 28%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly).
